<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Memory Preamble Tail Mechanism</title>
  <subtitle>System embeds most recent N facts in preamble, older facts available via MCP tool</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>MEMORY_PREAMBLE_TAIL controls how many recent facts are embedded directly in session preamble</fact>
    <fact>Older facts beyond tail size are listed as topic indexes only, fetched on-demand via list_facts MCP tool</fact>
    <fact>Setting value to 0 embeds all facts in preamble, useful for users with few facts</fact>
    <fact>Default configuration is 30 facts, currently set to 15 in both .env and .env.example</fact>
    <fact>Additional setting controls whether topic indexes for older facts are appended after recent facts</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The telegram-kiro-bridge implements a two-tier memory system for session preambles. The MEMORY_PREAMBLE_TAIL setting determines how many of the most recent facts are embedded directly into the preamble when a new session starts. Facts older than this threshold are not fully embedded but are instead represented as topic indexes, which the agent can fetch on-demand using the list_facts MCP tool. This approach balances context richness with token efficiency. Users with small fact databases can set the value to 0 to embed everything. The system also has a companion setting to control whether older fact topic indexes are appended to the preamble.</narrative>

    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>
